name: tailtest
description: Automatically generates and runs tests alongside your Codex session. Any language. No commands. No setup.

interface.shortDescription: Auto-generates and runs tests on every Codex turn
interface.longDescription: Tailtest watches every file Codex edits in your session and queues it for test generation at end of turn. It writes production-shaped scenarios using the R1-R15 rule layer, runs them, and surfaces only failures. Works with Python, JavaScript/TypeScript, Java, Kotlin, C#, Go, Rust, and Ruby out of the box. Supports an adversarial test mode that biases scenarios toward breakage paths (8 categories: boundary inputs, format/injection, type confusion, concurrent state, time/locale edges, partial failures, resource exhaustion, off-by-one logic). No prompting, no commands; runs automatically as Codex works.
defaultPrompt: Write tests for the file I just edited
Run an adversarial scan on my last change
Show tailtest summary for this session
